title: Back Up Important Files
summary: Copy important files to a second location so they are not lost.
learnerPromise: You will be able to choose which files to back up, copy them to a second location, and verify that they can be restored.
whyItMatters: Devices can break, get lost, or be stolen. Having a backup means your important documents, photos, and files are safe and can be recovered.
realLifeUses: Your phone is lost or broken and you need your photos and contacts on a new device
Your computer crashes and you need to recover your school or work documents
You accidentally delete an important file and need to get it back
You are resetting your device and want to keep your personal files safe
materialsNeeded: A second storage location: cloud storage account, USB drive, or external hard drive
result: At least one important file copied to a second location and successfully opened from that location.
youWillLearn: How to identify which files are most important to back up
How to copy files to a cloud storage service or external drive
How to verify that the backup worked by opening the copied file
How to set up automatic backups for future protection
miniChallenge: Choose three important files, copy them to a second location, and open one from the backup to confirm it works.
steps: Identify three files that would be hard to replace — such as photos, documents, or contacts
Choose a backup location: cloud storage, a USB drive, or an external hard drive
Copy each file to the backup location using upload, drag-and-drop, or a backup tool
Open one of the backed-up files from the new location to make sure it works correctly
If available, turn on automatic backup for photos or documents so future files are protected
completionCriteria: At least three important files are stored in a second location
You opened one file from the backup and confirmed it is intact and readable
commonProblems: Backup takes a long time with large files — start with your most important small files
Not enough space in cloud storage — check your available space or use a USB drive instead
Forgot where the backup is saved — write down the location or keep backups in a consistent place
tips: The 3-2-1 rule is a good guideline: keep 3 copies, on 2 types of storage, with 1 offsite
Turn on automatic photo and document backup in your cloud storage app so you do not have to remember
Label your external drives or USB sticks with the backup date so you know how recent they are
Back up before traveling or before major device updates
